name: Timberland
description_short: Timberland Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering checking, savings, auto loans, mortgages, credit cards, and business lending with competitive rates.
description_long:
Timberland Federal Credit Union (TFCU) is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union serving its local community. As a federally chartered institution with routing number 231380405, it operates under NCUA insurance and maintains multiple branch locations. The organization emphasizes accessibility and member benefits through various financial wellness programs and community initiatives.

TFCU offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business financial products. Personal services include checking and savings accounts (Timber Loyalty Checking, Timber Saver Account, Share Savings, Money Market), certificates of deposit (Share Certificates), IRAs, auto loans, home mortgage loans, home equity loans, credit cards with balance transfer options, private student loans, personal term loans, and loan protection products. Their vehicle financing includes a proprietary "Timber Drive" program featuring monthly payments up to 40% lower than conventional financing. Business members can access commercial lending, instant merchant accounts (IMA), and dedicated business services.

Timberland distinguishes itself through member-focused programs including free digital banking platforms with mobile check deposit, bill pay, fund transfers, and credit score monitoring via SavvyMoney. The credit union emphasizes financial wellness through partnerships like GreenPath Financial Counseling (offered at no cost to members), financial reality fairs, and youth savings programs (Timber Kids Savings Account, My First Nest Egg). A referral program called "Timber Together" and community scholarship initiatives demonstrate commitment beyond transactional banking. The organization maintains competitive rates and highlights surcharge-free ATM access.

TFCU represents a legitimate credit union option for consumers seeking member-owned banking with competitive products. However, as a regional institution, membership eligibility may be limited to specific geographic areas or employer groups. The website lacks transparency regarding membership requirements, account minimum balances, fee structures, and specific APR ranges for loans and credit cards. Consumers should verify membership eligibility and compare rates directly against national credit unions and banks before committing.
services: Personal checking accounts (Timber Loyalty Checking, Timber Hero, Share Draft Checking)
Personal savings accounts (Timber Saver Account, Share Savings, Money Market, Share Certificates, IRAs)
Auto loans and Timber Drive financing program (up to 40% lower payments)
Home mortgage loans and home equity loans
Credit cards with balance transfer options
Private student loans and personal term loans
Business commercial lending and instant merchant accounts (IMA)
Digital banking platform with mobile check deposit, bill pay, and fund transfers
Free credit score monitoring via SavvyMoney
GreenPath Financial Wellness counseling at no cost
Vehicle, boat, RV, motorcycle, and powersport financing
pros: Timber Drive program offers auto financing with monthly payments up to 40% lower than conventional financing
Free GreenPath Financial Wellness counseling and planning services available to all members
Digital banking with mobile check deposit, bill pay, fund transfers, and account management from anywhere
Free credit score monitoring through SavvyMoney via online and mobile banking
Surcharge-free ATM network access for members
Comprehensive product suite including auto loans, mortgages, home equity, credit cards, and business lending
Member referral program (Timber Together) and community scholarship initiatives
cons: Membership eligibility criteria not clearly disclosed on website—may be restricted by geography or employer
No specific APR ranges, fees, or account minimum balances disclosed on publicly accessible website content
Limited transparency on loan approval criteria, interest rate factors, or competitive rate comparisons
Regional credit union with multiple physical branches may limit accessibility for remote/out-of-area members
Website lacks detailed product disclosures and terms that would help consumers compare before applying
best_for: Local community members and employees within TFCU's service area seeking member-owned banking with competitive rates
Auto buyers interested in the Timber Drive financing program for lower monthly payments
Members prioritizing free financial wellness counseling and credit score monitoring tools
Small business owners seeking local commercial lending relationships and merchant services
diagnosis: Timberland FCU is best for consumers within its service area seeking member-owned banking with competitive auto financing (Timber Drive) and free financial wellness resources. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is unstated and likely restricted geographically or by employment, and the website provides insufficient rate and fee transparency to enable informed comparison shopping before application.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
